product_updates.title: Anthropic’s latest feud with the Trump admin may actually help it, sales data suggests
product_updates.content: Anthropic's popularity with business users is growing so well that the latest beef with the government might actually boost it, data from Ramp suggests.

product_updates.title: SpaceX valuation balloons to $2.6T, briefly passes Amazon
product_updates.content: SpaceX's valuation has increased by $1 trillion since its shares started trading on Friday.

product_updates.title: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
product_updates.content: Google has released Android 17 and Wear OS 7, introducing new multitasking features, parental controls, security tools, and smartwatch upgrades. The launch is also accompanied by a Pixel Drop that brings Google’s latest AI models to its devices.

product_updates.title: Sixty percent of US consumers say ‘AI’ in brand messaging is a turnoff, survey finds
product_updates.content: WordPress VIP’s latest survey suggests consumers are wary of AI-generated answers even as companies increasingly view AI search as an important referral channel.

product_updates.title: SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
product_updates.content: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

product_updates.title: DOJ claims xAI’s unpermitted gas turbines are a matter of ‘national, economic, and energy security’
product_updates.content: The Justice department says the Pentagon needs xAI to keep using its unpermitted gas turbines.

product_updates.title: Plaud says its software business topped $100M in ARR after shipping over 2M AI notetakers
product_updates.content: Plaud is trying to make a mark in a crowded market full of AI-powered meeting notetakers.

product_updates.title: Robinhood’s note on 10% layoffs shows blaming AI isn’t cutting it
product_updates.content: Unlike many of his tech industry peers who have cut thousands of jobs citing the need to restructure to make the most of AI, Robinhood's CEO Vlad Tenev conspicuously made no mention of AI in his note about layoffs.

product_updates.title: Probably raises $9M to build a more reliable kind of AI
product_updates.content: Probably wants to prevent hallucinations and factual errors from reaching users, and achieve accuracy on par with deterministic systems.

product_updates.title: SpaceX to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after blockbuster IPO
product_updates.content: The deal is supposed to help SpaceX's struggling AI division. The company told IPO investors it sees a $26 trillion addressable market in AI.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Year of free HPE software a “step in the correct direction” in VMware rivalry
technical_breakthroughs.content: Partner tells Ars that HPE should be giving out more free VM Essentials licenses.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning
technical_breakthroughs.content: UK government partners with Google DeepMind to build a new AI-powered prototype aimed at faster housing decisions.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Exclusive eBook: How AI is becoming the next military advisor
technical_breakthroughs.content: A collection of stories about how militaries are using AI models to make decisions. This subscriber-only eBook is a package of six stories that were originally published in MIT Technology Review between April 11, 2025, and April 21, 2026, and have been updated to reflect recent developments. by J…

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: the first brain implant power user and South Korea’s AI obsession
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. This man with ALS is the first “power user” of a brain implant that lets him speak Casey Harrell has had a set of electrodes embedded in his brain for almost…

technical_breakthroughs.title: Critical Copilot vulnerability allowed hackers to steal 2FA code from users
technical_breakthroughs.content: SearchLeak exploit shows why the industry's approach to LLM security fails over and over.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Want to get a data center online quickly? Give it some flex.
technical_breakthroughs.content: At the end of a tense and scoreless first half of a soccer match between the English men’s team and rival Germany, millions of Brits let out a collective sigh and did what they so often do in moments of stress: They made tea. That wave of electric kettles clicking on, however, caused a different…
